text.title: Test as Living Documentation Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Test as Living Documentation. Each rule pairs a binary check with a cited rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Use Given/When/Then or equivalent. Tests read as English sentences. Avoid programming jargon in test names.
rule.rationale: Programming-jargon test names exclude business reviewers; English sentences include them.

rule.statement: Test names mirror AC sentences. Tooling links test to AC ID (e.g., 'AC-123: user can recover password').
rule.rationale: Linking AC ↔ test creates traceability; uncited tests drift from intent.

rule.statement: Test runs publish a human-readable report (Cucumber HTML, custom doc). Stakeholders read it, not raw code.
rule.rationale: Generated docs are the deliverable; raw test code is for devs.

rule.statement: Track AC coverage: % AC with ≥1 test. Below 80% = AC reviewed but unimplemented in tests.
rule.rationale: Without coverage tracking, AC silently lose tests over time.

rule.statement: Quarterly: stakeholder reviews the generated docs. Confirms tests still represent the product.
rule.rationale: Tests + AC drift over time; quarterly review syncs them with business reality.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if every `## Applies If` entry holds and no `## Skip If` entry triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
